title: ludwigia palustris
intro: Ludwigia palustris, also known as marsh cayenne or water purslane, is a perennial herb native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of Ludwigia palustris include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
